title: Intense Slow Cooked Korean Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Australian dish featuring slow-cooked lamb in a ceramic tagine with ssamjang, perfect for a solo dinner. This dairy-free friendly recipe is made with fermented ingredients and has a molten texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together ssamjang, gochujang, soy sauce, and brown sugar.
Season the lamb shoulder with salt and black pepper.
Heat the sesame oil in the ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, then remove from the tagine.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the tagine and cook until fragrant.
Add the ssamjang mixture to the tagine and stir to combine.
Return the lamb shoulder to the tagine and spoon some of the ssamjang mixture over the top.
Cover the tagine and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2.5 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
